topic: A student discovers their teacher follows them on social media
raw_output:
[story 1]

The notification blinked on my phone screen, a familiar chime that usually signaled a mundane update, but this time, it was different. A profile picture I recognized instantly—Mr. Harrison, my notoriously stern but brilliant history teacher—was tagged in a post about a local historical society event. My heart gave a sudden, unexpected lurch, a warm flutter in my chest. I stared at the screen, my thumb hovering over the 'view profile' button. It wasn't a grand revelation, just a small digital thread connecting us, a tiny crack in the carefully constructed distance between us. I felt a strange lightness settle over me, as if a weight I hadn't realized I was carrying had just lifted. I reread the caption, a small, tentative smile playing on my lips. Maybe this was a sign that the walls he built around himself weren't as impenetrable as I'd always assumed.

[story 2]

I was scrolling through my feed during a study break, mindlessly consuming updates from friends, when I saw it. A story from Mr. Harrison's account—a candid photo taken at a recent conference, laughing with other academics. My breath hitched. For a moment, the noise of the library faded away. I leaned closer to the screen, my fingers tracing the outline of his profile picture. A distinct warmth spread through my palms, chasing away a lingering chill from the afternoon. I quickly pulled my hand back, my cheeks feeling suddenly flushed. I wasn't sure what this meant, only that a quiet sense of possibility was stirring inside, a gentle urging to perhaps say hello, to bridge the gap between the classroom and this digital space.

[story 3]

It happened entirely by accident. I was searching for an old lecture slide online, and there, tucked away in the search results, was his personal Instagram. Seeing his recent posts—a picture of a hiking trail, a thoughtful quote about perseverance—made my chest feel strangely expansive. A slow, steady feeling began to build in my core, a deep sense of affirmation. I didn't immediately click on anything; instead, I just sat there, absorbing the casual glimpse into his life. A soft, almost giddy sensation bubbled up, making my shoulders relax. It felt like an opening, a permission slip to look a little closer, to believe that perhaps the connection I’d always sensed was real, waiting just beyond the surface of the syllabus.
